I have completed the tasks as requested in the training scenario:

1.  **Modified the author** of the current working copy commit to `Trainer <trainer@example.com>` using the `jj metaedit --author "Trainer <trainer@example.com>"` command.
2.  **Added a template alias** named `training_log` to the global `jj` configuration at `~/.jjconfig.toml`. The template is defined as:
    ```toml
    [template-aliases]
    'training_log' = 'commit_id.short() ++ " - " ++ author.email() ++ " - " ++ description.first_line() ++ "\n"'
    ```
3.  **Generated a log** of the current commit (`@`) and its parent (`@-`) using the new `training_log` template and saved it to `/home/user/repo/formatted_log.txt`.

The resulting `/home/user/repo/formatted_log.txt` contains:
```
8b95305f333b - trainer@example.com - WIP commit
5181f643c1e4 - default@example.com - Base commit
```
